mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0% during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return with p-value less than 0.10 over 20-day horizons in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation above 230.0, unexpected CPI prints trigger immediate repricing of monetary policy expectations, causing short-term market volatility and negative 5-day forward returns as investors anticipate aggressive central bank tightening.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 70% of high-inflation observations, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which correlates with higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 24-month window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of industrial expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain exceeding 150,000 signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, supporting equity valuations and producing positive forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150.0 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields negative mean returns in more than 60% of triggered months during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, the resulting stagflationary pressure erodes corporate profit margins and forces restrictive monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.05 over rolling 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate environment, further declines or maintenance of the federal funds rate below 0.5 percent sustains liquidity-driven asset allocation flows into equities, generating positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn negative in more than 65% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in walk-forward tests.
